title: helianthemum variabile var. polifolium
intro: Helianthemum variabile var. polifolium, also known as Rockrose, is a flowering plant species that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its pink to purple flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which have been employed to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, contributing to its potential in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Rockrose is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
